From the Field

News & Stories

Stories of impact, research findings, and news from the creative economy communities we serve.

Featured Story
Report Featured

How Creative Districts Are Revitalizing Rust Belt Cities

Five years into our partnership with three Midwestern cities, the data is clear: investing in arts infrastructure pays dividends far beyond the cultural sector.

By Maria Chen Read more →
Story February 24, 2026

Meet Jamal: From Food Truck to Brick-and-Mortar

Through NACED's Accelerator program, chef and entrepreneur Jamal Washington built the business acumen to open his first restaurant — and he's not stopping there.

By Lisa Park Read more →
Announcement January 31, 2026

NACED Releases 2025 State of Creative Economies Report

Our flagship annual report documents the health of creative economies across 48 communities, revealing both encouraging trends and persistent challenges.

By NACED Staff Read more →
News January 14, 2026

What the 2025 NEA Budget Cuts Mean for Creative Entrepreneurs

An honest analysis of recent federal arts funding reductions — and what artists and community organizations can do to adapt and advocate.

By Dr. Sarah Kim Read more →
Report December 9, 2025

The Hidden Economy of Craft Fairs: New Data

A new NACED study reveals that craft and maker markets generate 3x the economic activity previously estimated — and most of it stays local.

By Maria Chen Read more →
Announcement November 19, 2025

NACED Welcomes Five New Board Members

We're thrilled to announce the addition of five outstanding leaders to the NACED board, bringing expertise in law, finance, urban planning, and the arts.

By NACED Staff Read more →
